title: Tragacantha uniflora

It belongs to the genus Crotalaria, which is a large group of plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. intro: Tragacantha uniflora, also known as Astragalus uniflorus, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha uniflora include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to boost the immune system and prevent illnesses.
